articles.title: Census portal shows Arunachal town as Chinese territory, glitch resolved
articles.description: The field operations for the Houselisting and Housing Census (HLO), constituting Phase I of the Census 2027, started April 16 across eight states and UTs, following the conclusion of the 15-day self-enumeration period.

articles.title: After women’s quota bill defeat, CM slams opposition
articles.description: In a passionate address, Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary expressed his outrage over the opposition's thwarting of the women's reservation bill, deeming it a 'dark stain on democracy.' He accused political adversaries, particularly Congress and RJD, of undermining the progress of women, vowing to hold them accountable at the polls.

articles.title: Operation Octopus 2.0: 52 arrested, including 32 bank officials, in pan-India cyber fraud crackdown by Hyderabad Police
articles.description: Hyderabad Police's Operation Octopus 2.0 arrests 52, including 32 bank officials, in a major cyber fraud crackdown across India.

articles.title: AI key to India's $30 trn goal if ecosystem builds in time: Umakant Soni
articles.description: AI could be key to India's $30 trillion goal by 2047, but a narrow three-to-five-year window will decide whether it builds its own ecosystem or remains dependent

articles.title: 'Do you eat onions?' PM Modi takes 'Jhalmuri break' during Bengal poll campaign - watch
articles.description: Amidst a high-stakes political campaign in West Bengal, Prime Minister Narendra Modi took a refreshing pause for a jhalmuri snack in Jhargram. He interacted with the vendor, inquiring about the price and confirming his liking for onions with a witty remark. This candid moment unfolded as the Prime Minister intensified his campaign efforts across the state.

articles.title: PM Modi’s 'jhalmuri break' steals show in West Bengal's Jhargram | Watch

articles.title: 'No One Can Deprive Us Of Our Nuclear Rights': Iran Says Wants To End War With Dignity
articles.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said Tehran is seeking to end the war “with dignity” while asserting that no country has the right to deprive Iran of its nuclear rights.

articles.title: Travel quote of the day by Sir Edmund Hillary: “It’s not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves”
articles.description: Sir Edmund Hillary's famous quote, "It’s not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves," highlights the internal battles faced by adventurers and travelers. The article emphasizes that true achievement lies in overcoming personal fears and self-doubt, rather than just external obstacles. This internal journey fosters growth, courage, and self-reliance.

articles.title: IPL 2026: Crisis-hit Kolkata Knight Riders brace for tough Rajasthan Royals test
articles.description: Kolkata Knight Riders are struggling at the halfway stage of the IPL with no wins, facing a daunting task to turn their season around. Skipper Ajinkya Rahane's leadership and confusing batting order decisions, particularly for Cameron Green, have drawn criticism. They now face a Rajasthan Royals side looking to rebound after a recent loss.

articles.title: Houthi rebels on high alert to confront "any aggression against the Yemeni people"
articles.description: Yemen's Houthi officials have issued strong warnings, with the Defence Minister stating rebels are on high alert for aggression. Deputy Foreign Minister Hussein al-Ezzi threatened to close the strategic Bab al-Mandeb Strait if US policies obstructing peace are not ended, impacting global shipping routes.

articles.title: Perfect Crown episodes 5-6 OTT release in India: When and where to watch IU and Byeon Woo-seok’s most-watched romantic Korean series? Plot, cast and more
articles.description: Perfect Crown, a new K-drama, is grabbing audiences' attention worldwide. Starring IU and Byeon Woo-seok, the series brings romance and royalty in a modern setting. New episodes are released weekly, with the finale scheduled for May 16. Fans are eagerly anticipating the upcoming episodes. Read on!

articles.title: What Dexter Lawrence blockbuster trade reveals about Bengals’ urgency and Giants’ long-term strategy shift
articles.description: Dexter Lawrence’s move from the Giants to the Bengals is a rare pre-draft trade that impacts both strategy and identity. New York gains flexibility with two top-10 picks but must replace a cornerstone defender. Cincinnati, meanwhile, prioritizes immediate defensive improvement over draft potential, signaling urgency around Joe Burrow’s prime years. The deal reflects contrasting team philosophies but a shared need for clarity before the draft.

articles.title: Veteran actor Nagarjuna maintains fit physique in 60s with 'intense' workouts 5-6 days a week and 14-hour fasting daily
articles.description: Telugu superstar Nagarjuna prioritises fitness and a disciplined diet. He works out almost every day, and believes in enjoying life with a weekly cheat day.

articles.description: Buckley Carlson, son of Tucker Carlson, has resigned as deputy press secretary to Vice President JD Vance amid family tensions with Trump.

articles.title: House of drug peddler demolished in Jammu

articles.title: ‘Fungus and Iron’ manga to end with volume 10 as anime adaptation nears
articles.description: Ayaka Katayama's acclaimed dystopian manga 'Fungus and Iron' is set to conclude with its 10th collected volume. The series, which began serialization in March 2021, has captivated readers with its blend of dark sci-fi and character drama. An anime adaptation was also recently announced, further increasing anticipation for the manga's finale.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Bill Gates: “It’s fine to celebrate success, but it is more important to heed the lessons of failure.”
articles.description: Bill Gates’ quote highlights that real learning comes from failure, feedback, and experience, not just success. It underscores the value of persistence, critical thinking, and continuous self-improvement in everyday life.

articles.title: Court sentences two to 5 Years jail in mobile snatching case
articles.description: The court directed that all sentences shall run concurrently. It also ordered that the period already spent in custody during investigation and trial be set off against the total sentence.

articles.title: Chartist Talk: Nifty may head to 25,000 if 24,700 breaks, says SBI Securities’ Sudeep Shah; shares top picks
articles.description: According to Sudeep Shah, the immediate resistance for the Nifty 50 is placed in the 24,650–24,700 zone. A decisive breakout above 24,700 could open the path towards 25,000 and subsequently 25,200.
